access.accessMethod: Read the checked-out CME submodule path
access.entityLabel: General Corpus source XML for CME00024

entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00024
notes: The file is 341,378 bytes at the recorded CME submodule commit.

Its TEMPHEAD records a review dated 2016-08-25 that added TEMPHEAD, proofed the title page, added previously missing beginning text keyed as M224, revised structure and note placement, and corrected page milestones; this does not establish the initial encoding date.
The representation contains 5 DIV1, 43 DIV2, 50 DIV3, 155 PB, 268 NOTE, and 222 P elements.

rights.basis: The exact U-M item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies the DPLA No Copyright–United States rights statement.

dateStatements.label: documented XML review
dateStatements.notes: The TEMPHEAD date documents later review and correction activity, not original keying or publication.

entities.description: A public structured digital text of Moore's selected documents, with page-oriented navigation and edition-derived headings and notes.
entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text CME00024
notes: The XML declares TEXT/@LANG as enm, but the encoded edition also contains substantive Latin bonds and receiver-account extracts plus modern English editorial matter.
It encodes the edition title page and printed pp. 1–154, including Part I, Part II, and the appendix, but omits the contents, Moore's introduction pp. i–xxvi, and index pp. 155–161.
The 43 DIV2 containers are encoding and editorial-unit metrics; they are not a count of independent physical documents.

rights.basis: The exact U-M item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies the DPLA No Copyright–United States rights statement.
rights.component: The U-M digital collection item as presented

bibliographic.citation: Letters and Papers of John Shillingford, Mayor of Exeter 1447–50, ed. Stuart A. Moore, Camden Society, New Series II ([Westminster]: Printed for the Camden Society, 1871).
bibliographic.title: Letters and Papers of John Shillingford, Mayor of Exeter 1447–50

entities.description: Moore's edited selection and arrangement of letters, petitions, pleadings, agreements, and municipal-account extracts concerning Exeter's civic and cathedral jurisdictional dispute.
entities.label: Stuart A. Moore's 1871 Letters and Papers of John Shillingford
notes: The title page says printed for the Camden Society in 1871; the verso identifies J. B. Nichols and Sons, Westminster, as printer.
Moore says the papers had been dispersed in Exeter's municipal archives and thanks the Mayor and Corporation for lending the originals.
The edition prints 43 top-level documentary units in three parts, but its Roman numerals omit XXIV, duplicate XXXV, and restart later sequences incorrectly.
Moore sometimes constructs one reading from several drafts, copies, fragments, or rolls and records only some variants and interventions in footnotes.

rights.basis: U-M applies No Copyright–United States to the exact 1871 item, and Wikimedia Commons labels the Internet Archive-derived scan PD-US-expired.
rights.component: The 1871 edition text, typography, and apparatus

access.format: PDF, 200 access pages; XML scan map; JSON item metadata

notes: Complete means the provider's exposed access PDF of the bound volume; six scan-data leaves excluded from access formats and unoffered spine or edge views are not present.

workPortion.label: CME00024 encoded collection: edition title page and printed documents pp. 1–154
locators: Internet Archive BookReader leaf 5 (edition title page)
printed pp. 1–154 = Internet Archive BookReader leaves 33–186
notes: The complete scan also contains the Camden Society preliminaries, Moore introduction pp. i–xxvi, index pp. 155–161, covers, endleaves, and blanks omitted from the XML.

Internet Archive item metadata snapshot for the Moore volume

rights.basis: The Commons file page identifies this Internet Archive item and direct PDF as its source and marks the derivative PD-US-expired; the Internet Archive metadata itself supplies no rights or license field.
rights.component: The public scan derivative of the 1871 volume
notes: The cached exact Internet Archive PDF and the separately hosted Commons PDF are not byte-identical, so the Commons label is preserved as a downstream assertion rather than an open license inferred for every IA file-layer asset.

entities.description: A complete public access scan of the bound printed volume contributed by University of California Libraries and digitized by Internet Archive in 2008; it is not a facsimile of the medieval archival documents.
entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Moore's 1871 volume
notes: The scan-data file records 206 capture leaves and 400 dpi; the access PDF contains 200 pages because leaves marked out of access formats are omitted.
Visual inspection confirmed the red front and rear boards, edition title page, ordinary text, work ending, index, and final blank.

dateStatements.notes: This is the series-level repository date; individual documents in Moore's selection are more narrowly dated where evidence permits.

entities.description: An aggregate archival witness comprising many separate letters, petitions, drafts, fragments, and rolls concerning the dispute over St Stephen's or the Bishop's Fee; it is not one codex or one authored work.

holding.notes: The current catalog divides the series into files ECA/1/1/6/1–4 at Devon Heritage Centre. Cruse used the older C.S. box and folder designations; an exact old-to-current and print-unit crosswalk has not been published.

entities.label: Devon Heritage Centre ECA/1/1/6, Shillingford letters and papers
notes: The current series record explicitly says that this material was printed in part in Moore's 1871 volume.
Moore's title and the archive's historical numbering do not prove that each printed unit equals one current physical document.

physicalDescription.extent: 3 archive boxes and 1 oversize box in the current catalog; a historical study reports 76 documents ranging from small fragments to multi-membrane rolls
physicalDescription.format: dispersed archival documents, drafts, fragments, folders, and rolls
notes: Cruse emphasizes that the archive does not form one discrete manuscript.
The current four file descriptions list numbered documents with gaps and an overlap at nos. 72–73, plus unnumbered drafts and fragments; they cannot be treated as a settled 76-item inventory.

rights.basis: Devon Archives limits supplied copies and researcher photographs to private research and requires written permission before publication, including online publication.

next_steps: Ask Devon Archives whether an internal item list or old-to-current concordance survives.
Compare every printed opening, endorsement, material note, and variant siglum against the originals under current references.
Reconcile the child-record gaps and overlap in document numbers before assigning item-level shelfmarks.
openQuestions.question: How do Moore's 43 printed documentary containers and source labels map item by item to current ECA/1/1/6 files /1–/4 and their older box, folder, and ECA/MR designations?

next_steps: Request quotations and condition assessments for complete ECA/1/1/6, all 17 membranes of ECA/11/MR/3, all 51 membranes of ECA/2/1/1/156, and every surface of ECA/3/1/1/1/99–104.
Keep private research-copy permission separate from publication and redistribution permission.
If supplied, map CME00024 portions separately and retain full-object coverage in the private cache.
openQuestions.question: Can Devon Archives supply condition-safe complete digital surrogates of all four physical source groups, including all adjacent and non-work material?

next_steps: Consult U-M collection production records or archived item metadata.
Do not infer manual keying, OCR, or initial publication date from the later TEMPHEAD review.
openQuestions.question: When and by what process was the U-M CME00024 transcription first created before the documented 2016 review?

next_steps: Treat 1447–50 as part of the established title rather than silently as the exact date range of every selected document.
Check a future item-level archive concordance and Moore's unencoded source notes for any intended 1450 endpoint.
openQuestions.question: Why does Moore's title use 1447–50 when the encoded and inspected documentary text labels account material through 1448–49 and concentrates the correspondence in 1447–48?

reviewNotes: The cataloging subject is Moore's 1871 edited documentary selection; the many historical documents, archival groupings, printed edition, U-M encoding, repository artifact, and scan remain separate layers.
The current ECA/1/1/6 child descriptions contain overlapping and missing document-number ranges, so they do not support an item-by-item crosswalk to Moore's printed numerals.
Complete-facsimile negatives refer only to public web access checked on 2026-07-12; the Devon catalog marks the originals open and the repository offers paid copying and permission routes.

The Internet Archive PDF is complete relative to the provider's exposed bound-volume access object; its scan-data inventory has 206 capture leaves but the PDF has 200 included pages, with front and rear boards and blanks but no separate spine or edge views.

lineage.summary: The checked-in CME00024 XML is a University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse encoding of Stuart A. Moore's 1871 Camden Society edited selection, not a transcription of one medieval codex or of the complete surviving Shillingford archive. It encodes the edition title page and the documentary text on printed pp. 1–154, arranged as 43 top-level document containers in three parts, while omitting the Camden preliminaries, Moore's introduction pp. i–xxvi, and the index pp. 155–161. Moore selected, transcribed, and sometimes conflated drafts, copies, fragments, and rolls now principally cataloged at Devon Heritage Centre as ECA/1/1/6; his appendix also excerpts ECA/11/MR/3 membrane 6, ECA/2/1/1/156 membrane 21, and six receiver's account rolls ECA/3/1/1/1/99–104. A complete public scan of the printed book and a complete supporting thesis are cached. No public complete digital surrogate of any of the four archival source groups was found on 2026-07-12; exact catalog, onsite, and mediated whole-object reproduction routes are recorded instead.

catalogingSubject.label: Letters and Papers of John Shillingford, Mayor of Exeter, 1447–50
catalogingSubject.scope_note: Stuart A. Moore's 1871 editorial selection and arrangement of documents associated with John Shillingford and Exeter's civic-cathedral dispute, represented in the repository by 43 top-level documentary containers in three parts. The cataloging subject is not the whole surviving Shillingford archive, not one manuscript or codex, and not necessarily 43 independent physical works: Moore collated drafts, copies, fragments, rolls, and separately cataloged appendix records. The historical documents, Moore's edition, the archival witnesses, U-M's electronic encoding, the repository XML, and the modern scan remain distinct lineage layers.

contentParts.label: Part I: Letters of John Shillingford
contentParts.summary: Twenty-eight top-level containers, chiefly Shillingford's late 1447–early 1448 letters and drafts concerning the Exeter dispute and his business in London.

contentParts.label: Part II: Petitions, articles, answers, and related documents
contentParts.summary: Eight top-level containers, subdivided into 44 articles or parts, presenting formal statements, petitions, responses, bonds, memoranda, and evidence in the civic-cathedral dispute.

contentParts.label: Appendix
contentParts.summary: Seven top-level containers of supplementary civic records, including an Exbridge petition, a Mayor's Court-roll extract, and six annual receiver-account extracts.

notes: The title's 1447–50 is retained as title information, not imposed as an exact content range. The six appendix accounts represented in the XML run from 22 to 28 Henry VI, approximately 1443–1449; the principal dispute correspondence is concentrated in late 1447 and early 1448.

extent.description: The repository XML contains 43 body DIV2 top-level documentary containers in three DIV1 parts, with 50 nested DIV3 units. Part I has 28 DIV2 units; Part II has eight DIV2 and 44 DIV3 units; the Appendix has seven DIV2 and six DIV3 units. These edition/encoding containers are not a physical-document count: Moore's Roman numbering is irregular, some units collate multiple drafts or fragments, and some appendix units excerpt larger rolls.

scope.description: Moore's title, introduction, headings, notes, expansions, and index

notes: The 43 top-level documentary containers are Moore/U-M structural units, not a count of distinct manuscripts or authored works.

The current Devon catalog exposes no public digitized attachments for the identified source records as of 2026-07-12; the Internet Archive PDF is a scan of Moore's printed edition, not a manuscript facsimile.

Complete reproduction requests for roll sources must include every membrane, side, endorsement, attachment, seal, label, fold, fragment, and adjacent record, then identify the printed work portion rather than requesting only the excerpted membrane.
The XML review note dated 2016-08-25 documents a later proofing and restructuring pass, not the original U-M keying or encoding date.

next_steps: Obtain or inspect complete images and folder-level inventories for all four ECA/1/1/6 child records, including unnumbered fragments, endorsements, and labels.
Build a many-to-many crosswalk that preserves Moore's conflations and current overlapping or missing catalog number ranges rather than forcing one-to-one matches.
openQuestions.question: How do Moore's 43 top-level editorial units, internal Roman numerals, and cited fragments map to the current ECA/1/1/6 child records and their document numbers?

next_steps: Record printed labels verbatim and assign separate stable corpus component identifiers.
Reconcile each label against headings, pagination, manuscript references, and Cruse's numbering analysis before exposing a corrected sequence.
openQuestions.question: What corrected editorial sequence should be used for item-level cataloging when Moore omits XXIV, prints the following unit as XXV, and later duplicates or restarts XXXV and XXXVI?

next_steps: Inspect every dated heading and Moore's source citations against complete manuscript images.
Continue to preserve 1447–50 as part of the edition title while displaying the inspected content range separately.
openQuestions.question: Which documentary or editorial rationale supports the title endpoint 1450 when the XML's six receiver-account extracts end in 1448–49 and the principal correspondence is chiefly 1447–48?

next_steps: Create item-level diplomatic descriptions from complete archival images, separately recording author, sender, issuer, scribe, recipient, language, material form, and documentary state.
Do not propagate Shillingford authorship or Middle English to every component from the collection title or U-M header.

subjects.label: Late medieval civic correspondence and documentary practice

summaries.value: Moore's edited collection assembles letters, petitions, pleadings, memoranda, legal instruments, and municipal accounts centered on John Shillingford's mayoralty and Exeter's dispute with the cathedral authorities over civic liberties and jurisdiction. The main correspondence follows Shillingford's efforts in Exeter and London in late 1447 and early 1448; the second part gathers formal articles, answers, bonds, and related records; the appendix adds supplementary civic documents and account extracts extending the represented chronology to approximately 1443–1449. The collection is especially important for late medieval urban government, civic advocacy, legal process, vernacular administrative writing, and the material practices of drafting and copying.

The encoded selection has three main parts. Part I, Letters of John Shillingford, contains 28 top-level documentary containers on printed pages 1–72. Part II contains eight top-level containers on pages 75–132, subdivided into 44 articles or parts for petitions, pleadings, bonds, and related records. The Appendix contains seven top-level containers on pages 133–154, including an Exbridge petition, an extract from a 1444–45 Mayor's Court roll, and six receiver-account extracts. The print volume also contains a contents list, Moore's introduction on pages i–xxvi, and an index on pages 155–161, which the repository XML omits.

This record describes Moore's 1871 editorially created selection as the corpus work. The 43 top-level XML containers describe edition structure, not 43 uniform or independent archival objects. Moore sometimes combined drafts, copies, fragments, or roll extracts, and the appendix draws from separately cataloged municipal series. Current Devon catalog ranges, Moore's Roman numerals, and earlier archive-box designations do not support a complete one-to-one item crosswalk, so document-level authorship, shelfmarks, dates, and languages are not inferred beyond inspected evidence.
